title: Econ regressions
tagline: Extract every regression from an empirical-economics paper, group by table, classify headlines, rate confidence per table
description: Empirical-economics regression extractor (v2 schema).  Each paper renders as one synthesised "Paper metadata" entry plus one entry per results table.  Each table-entry shows the table-level metadata (caption, page, panels, notes) above an HTML table of the table's regression-columns side-by-side — rows are properties (dependent var / model / FEs / controls / SEs / classification / headline reasoning), columns are the regression-columns from the paper.  Per-table extraction_confidence + per-cell evidence highlighting.
task_description: Identify every regression-results table in the paper (main body + appendix + online appendix) and extract one entry per regression-column, with everything needed to rerun the regression given access to the underlying data.
context: Empirical-economics workflow for primary studies that report regression results in tables (typical AEJ / AER / QJE format).  One paper → one paper-metadata entry + one entry per table; each table-entry contains every regression-column from that table laid out as an HTML table.
